title: Founding Engineer
description: Gontrel is live in Dublin with 10,000 downloads and a team in place. Now we need a founding engineer to own what comes next. Gontrel is a food discovery app, live in Dublin, over 10,000 downloads and growing. The idea is simple. Imagine TikTok but for food. You open the app, you see great video content from restaurants and food creators near you, and you find somewhere to eat that you actually get excited about. We're a small team and we move fast. We ship twice a week. The person we bring in here will have a real say in how the product is built. That's genuinely what this role is. What you'll be working on: * The recommendation engine: Helping users find content they love, faster. * Video infrastructure: Upload, processing, playback. It needs to feel smooth and it needs to work every time. * Creator tools: Making it easy for restaurants and food creators to post content, grow an audience, and get rewarded for it. * Discovery: Search, location, the stuff that connects the right person to the right place at the right moment. Stack: Node.js, AWS, Swift (iOS). You'll touch a bit of everything. Who we're looking for: Ideally we are looking for someone who has shipped a consumer app before. Someone who knows what it feels like to build something, put it in front of real people, watch what happens, and go again. That whole cycle. If that's you, we want to hear from you straight away. If you haven't done consumer apps, that's okay too. We'd just want to see something else that shows us you're the kind of person who loves building and shipping things that actually get used. You'll really enjoy this if you love the early stages, like to ship things and go again, care about how things feel to use, and want to own something not just contribute to it. What's in it for you: * Equity. You'll own a piece of the company. * Compensation. Competitive, we'll talk numbers directly. * Location. In person, Dublin office. How to apply: * We keep it simple. There's a short technical test linked here (5 days to complete): https://docs.google.com/document/d/1X5Cgepb5\_qvLOGz\-7xSc7\-48Mkm5hqo4HG7hHDGVAAU/edit?usp\=sharing. * Have a look at it and send your completed submission to info@gontrel.com. Already built something in Swift? Skip the test and just send us the app instead.

title: Fraud Analyst
description: **Fraud Analyst\- Dublin Ireland ( Hybrid) Contract Role\- 3 days a week onsite in South Dublin** We're seeking a detail\-oriented Fraud Analyst to join our team. This role offers an excellent opportunity to develop expertise in digital fraud detection, data analysis, and cybersecurity. **The Role:** You'll begin by supporting quality assurance and reporting on fraud alerts, ensuring consistency with procedures. As you gain experience, you'll transition into investigating fraud events, identifying emerging threats, and helping improve detection and prevention strategies. **Key Responsibilities** * Perform QA on daily fraud alerts * Produce regular reports on alert volumes and QA results * Investigate abnormal activity and potential threats * Identify fraud trends and recommend prevention measures * Stay current on malware and security vulnerabilities * Communicate findings and risks to stakeholders **Required Skills** * Strong communication and teamwork skills * Excellent organizational and analytical abilities * Experience working with large data sets and data analysis tools * Ability to identify anomalies and extract insights from data * Understanding of common application security vulnerabilities **Desired Skills** * Experience with Splunk or similar tools * Familiarity with SIEM, EDR, or threat intelligence platforms * Knowledge of web application security or development * Scripting/programming experience (e.g., Python, SQL) **Berkley Business \& Technology is a specialist consultancy, recruiting professionals for organizations throughout Ireland, UK \& Europe; for more information go to** **www.berkley\-group.com** **.** **If you would like to discuss this vacancy further in confidence, please send your profile to jburke@berkley\-group.com**

title: Fraud Analyst
description: **Fraud Review Analyst \| Fully Remote \| Shift Based** Every platform that handles money, claims, or user trust has a problem. The problem is fraud. And behind every good fraud operation, there are sharp, detail\-oriented people quietly making the calls that protect real users from real harm. This is one of those roles. A well\-established global platform is growing their team ahead of their summer peak, and they are looking for a Fraud Review Analyst to join them. The team reviews suspicious claims and activity, working through a caseload of cases each day using a combination of manual investigation and data analysis. It is methodical, meaningful work that requires people who know how to read signals, connect the dots and make sound decisions under pressure. **ABOUT THE ROLE** You will work through a daily caseload, investigating suspicious activity at the account level. That might mean reviewing documentation, identifying connections between users, spotting fabricated details or examining payment anomalies. You will be supported through a structured two\-week onboarding programme before your caseload builds. Average case closure sits at around ten days, and the team works closely with colleagues across the wider business. **ABOUT THE PERSON** Experience spotting fraudulent signals in any of the following areas: * Insurance claims or case management * Fraud payments or financial crime * Trust and safety, from platforms such as PayPal, TikTok, Meta or similar * Document verification or identity fraud Tooling experience is a bonus but not a barrier. They will train you on their internal systems regardless. **The practicalities** This is a shift\-based, fully remote role. The preferred schedule is Tuesday to Saturday, 9 am to 6 pm (Irish time), with some flexibility on start time. A Sunday to Thursday pattern is the second preference. Office space is available if you want it, but no one is expecting you to use it. If this sounds like the kind of work you have been doing or the direction you want to move in, get in touch with Jane at Barden, and she will take it from there.

title: Global Mobility Specialist
description: Because we strive to put people first. Culture, our way. People and Culture at Primark What’s a career at Primark all about? The positive impact you’re making, the experiences you’re having and the people you’re with. You’re our inspiration. Embrace what sets you apart, own your career and develop in ways you never expected. Leave your mark. And do it your way. Our team has created a workplace of inspiration and growth. Be a part of building an inclusive and empowering culture, where every colleague’s unique abilities are recognised and celebrated. Collaborate with a team committed to the wellbeing of our workforce and define what it means to work at Primark. What You’ll Do as Global Mobility Specialist FTC 12 month contract In your role, you’ll collaborate with a wide range of people across the business. Here’s a flavour of your day\-to\-day: * Provide expert end‑to‑end Global Mobility support, including pre‑assignment planning, cost estimates, documentation, repatriation coordination, supplier management, and delivering exceptional assignee experiences while partnering with payroll, tax, legal, finance, and other stakeholders. * Interpret and apply mobility policies, handle complex or unique cases, act as an escalation point for policy guidance, and recommend improvements to the Global Mobility Manager. * Support and guide junior mobility team members, lead on best‑practice approaches for relocation and immigration cases and ensure seamless coordination of logistical aspects for international moves. * Serve as primary contact for assignees and senior colleagues, conducting pre‑assignment briefings, advising on COLA, tax equalisation, gross‑ups, social security, and validating/authorising cost projections. * Manage immigration processes, including initial consultations with candidates/assignees, reviewing immigration backgrounds, coordinating applications/appointments, and advising internal teams on compliant hiring or relocation approaches. * Monitor vendor performance and budgets, oversee SLAs, escalate service issues, manage assignment documentation and analytics, lead year‑end payroll and reporting requirements, and ensure accurate tracking of all mobility‑related records and metrics. What You’ll Get People are at the heart of what we do here, so it’s essential we provide you with the right environment to perform at your very best. Let’s talk lifestyle: Let’s talk lifestyle: Healthcare, pension, and potential bonus. 27 days of leave, plus bank holidays and if you want, you can buy 5 more. Because Primark is all about tailoring to you, we offer Tax Saver Tickets, fitness centre, and a subsidised cafeteria. What You’ll Bring Here at Primark, we want everyone to feel valued – so please bring your authentic self to work, of course with some other key experience and abilities for this role in particular: * Proactively tracks mobility trends, engages in industry networking, and recommends policy and process enhancements to continuously improve mobility practices. * Excellent written and verbal communication, with strong interpersonal, planning, and client‑service skills, able to explain complex mobility topics clearly, compliantly, and with discretion. * Highly adaptable, solution‑focused, and flexible, thriving in fast‑paced environments, taking on new tasks as needed, and adjusting to evolving business demands with professionalism and integrity. * Advanced MS Office capability (Excel, PowerPoint, Word) and strong overall IT awareness, with exceptional attention to detail, accuracy, and a “right‑first‑time” mindset. * Collaborative and relationship‑driven, building strong rapport across internal teams, working effectively with others, and operating with diplomacy, tact, and confidentiality. * Fully tax‑qualified or holding an equivalent qualification (e.g., Accountancy) with 4–7 year’s relevant Global Mobility or Reward experience, demonstrating willingness to learn and the ability to independently plan and manage workload to achieve objectives. Does this sound like you? Great, because we can’t wait to see what you’ll bring. You’ll be supported within a team of equally capable people, celebrating who you are and aiding you reach your potential. At Primark, we’re excited about our future \- and we’re excited to develop yours. **About Primark** At Primark, people matter. They’re the beating heart of our business and the reason we’ve grown from our first store in Dublin in 1969 to a £9bn\+ turnover business and over 80,000 colleagues and over 440 stores in 17 countries today. Our values run through everything we do. In essence, we're Caring and always strive to put people first. We're also Dynamic, bravely pushing the boundaries to stay ahead. And finally, we succeed Together. title: Governance, Risk and Compliance Graduate - 2026 Graduate Programme (5543)

We also value: * We're looking for graduates with Level 8 or above qualification in Business, Management, Information Technology, Computer Science, or a related field. * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills. * Excellent attention to detail and organizational skills. * Strong communication and report\-writing skills. * Ability to work collaboratively in a team environment. * Basic understanding of ISO standards, Data Privacy and Digital regulatory requirements. * Time management skills to meet project deadlines. * Preferable to have an operational and/or security\-related certification * Adaptability and eagerness to learn and develop professionally. * Ability to engage with clients and partners in developing project requirements. **Please submit a copy of your CV and Transcripts when submitting an application** **Please submit a copy of your CV and Transcripts when submitting an application**

title: Graduate QAQC Engineer - 2026
description: **About Us** Winthrop is a dedicated Data Centre Construction company providing turnkey solutions to our clients, headquartered in Dublin and delivering throughout Europe. Winthrop specialises in providing Turnkey Data Centre Solutions, providing services from design, through to construction across the full range of civil, structural, architectural, mechanical and electrical services as well as commissioning. **Winthrop Graduate Programme:** Building on the success of our comprehensive Graduate Programme introduced in 2022, we offer an opportunity to work on cutting\-edge projects across Ireland and Europe. This enables graduates to gain valuable technical experience during a crucial stage in their careers. Our programme is uniquely designed to develop skilled professionals with a deep understanding of all phases of large\-scale construction projects, providing graduates with exposure to the complete construction process. **About the Role** We are seeking a detail\-oriented **QA/QC Engineer Graduate** to join our teams in Europe and gain hands\-on experience in quality management across large\-scale construction projects. This role offers exposure to quality assurance and control processes, ensuring compliance with project specifications, standards, and regulatory requirements. **Responsibilities** * Assist in developing and implementing QA/QC plans and procedures * Support inspection and testing activities for materials and workmanship * Review and interpret technical drawings and specifications to ensure compliance * Maintain accurate quality documentation, including inspection reports and test records * Liaise with site teams, subcontractors, and suppliers to resolve quality issues * Participate in root cause analysis and corrective action plans for non\-conformances * Assist in internal audits and quality reviews * Promote a culture of quality and continuous improvement across the project team **Requirements** * **Education:** Bachelor's degree in Mechanical, Electrical, Civil Engineering, or a related discipline * Graduation between 2025\-2026 * Knowledge of quality standards and processe **s** (ISO 9001 is an advantage) * Strong attention to detail and analytical skills * Excellent communication and organizational skills * Proficient in MS Office; familiarity with QA/QC software or systems is a plus * Commitment to health, safety, and quality standards **Benefits** * **Mentorship from experienced professionals** , supporting you throughout your graduate programme and beyond * **Exposure to large‑scale data centre projects** , gaining hands‑on international experience from the outset * **A competitive salary, with an additional package to support working and living abroad** * **A collaborative and supportive work culture** , where graduates are invested in as long‑term team members * **A clear pathway from the graduate programme into a permanent role** , offering long‑term career stability and ongoing progression with the business ***Winthrop Technologies Ltd. is an equal opportunities employer***

title: Graduate Supply Chain Coordinator
description: **Job Title: Graduate Supply Chain Coordinator** **Job Location: Dublin City South** **Salary: DOE \+ bonus \& benefits.** Are you a recent graduate ready to launch your career in a fast\-paced, dynamic environment? We’re looking for an enthusiastic and driven individual to join our Customer Operations team as a Graduate Supply Chain Coordinator. This is an exciting opportunity to gain hands\-on experience in supply chain, procurement, and customer delivery within a growing business. **About the Role** In this role, you’ll be at the centre of our vehicle supply chain—supporting the end\-to\-end procurement and delivery process. From placing orders to coordinating with suppliers and ensuring vehicles reach customers on time, you’ll develop valuable experience in operations, logistics, and stakeholder management. **What You’ll Be Doing** * Manage the lifecycle of vehicle orders from placement through to delivery * Coordinate with suppliers and customers to ensure timely and accurate deliveries * Monitor delivery schedules and proactively manage any delays * Support supplier negotiations to achieve competitive pricing and value * Coordinate vehicle fit\-outs with third\-party providers * Oversee vehicle registrations in line with customer and contract requirements * Manage vehicle returns alongside new deliveries * Build and maintain strong relationships with suppliers and manufacturers * Track performance, maintain accurate systems, and support reporting * Assist in the planning and delivery of annual vehicle supply tenders * Collaborate with internal teams to ensure customer and business objectives are met **What We’re Looking For** * Strong organisational and time management skills * A proactive, self\-motivated approach with a willingness to learn * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills * Good analytical and problem\-solving abilities * Proficiency in Microsoft Office, especially Excel * Ability to multitask and work in a fast\-paced environment * A customer\-focused mindset * Previous experience or interest in supply chain, logistics, or the motor industry is an advantage, but not essential **Why Join Us?** * A structured and supportive environment for graduates * Real responsibility and meaningful project exposure from day one * Opportunities for career progression in supply chain, procurement, and operations * A collaborative team culture where your ideas are valued If you’re motivated, curious, and ready to build a strong foundation for your career, please apply via the link to Emily Mason.

title: IT Helpdesk Engineer
company: MCS Group | Your Specialist Recruitment Consultancy
description: **Job Title:** IT Support Engineer **Location:** Dublin (On\-site) **Salary:** Up to €45,000 \+ bonus \+ many other amazing benefits About the Company We're working with a long\-standing Dublin\-based business that has built a strong reputation over the years and continues to grow. They're a team of 250\+ people, working on large, high\-profile projects with some really well\-known global clients. It's a busy, fast\-moving environment, but one that genuinely puts a lot of emphasis on culture, collaboration, and keeping things enjoyable day to day. From a tech perspective, they've invested well and continue to evolve, so you're not stepping into something stale or overly rigid. The Opportunity This is the kind of IT Support role people often say they want, but don't always find. Yes, there are tickets. But this isn't a "sit behind a screen and close tickets all day" type job. You'll be out on the floor, speaking to people, solving problems in real time, and becoming a go\-to person across the business. The role naturally gives you a lot of visibility, and that's something the team really values. You'll be joining a small IT team where everyone gets stuck in. There's a good mix of day\-to\-day support and exposure to wider systems and projects, so you're constantly learning rather than just repeating the same tasks. It's a great move for someone who feels like they've outgrown a standard service desk role and wants something a bit more hands\-on and engaging. What You'll Be Doing You'll be supporting the wider business with whatever comes up day to day \- from user issues and device setups to getting involved in broader fixes and improvements. Some days will be busier than others (roughly 15\-30 tickets between the team), but the key difference here is how the team works together. It's collaborative, you're not left on your own, and there's a shared responsibility to get things sorted quickly. A big part of the role is also how you interact with people. You'll be dealing with everyone from junior staff to senior leaders, so being able to explain things clearly and build trust is just as important as your technical ability. What They're Looking For * Around 3\-5 years' experience in IT Support * Someone comfortable working in a fast\-paced environment * Solid fundamentals across Microsoft technologies and general troubleshooting * Most importantly, someone who enjoys working with people and being visible in the business They're not overly focused on certifications. If you want to pursue them, they'll support you, but attitude and approach matter more here. How This Role Works This is an on\-site role, and that's intentional. Because of how hands\-on and people\-facing the role is, being in the office is a big part of it. There is flexibility to work from home one day a week once you've settled in, but day\-to\-day you'll be in the mix with the wider team. What's on Offer Salary is up to €45,000, alongside a solid benefits package including bonus, pension, learning support, and a genuinely social working environment. It's the kind of place where people know each other, get involved, and enjoy coming into work \- which makes a big difference in a role like this. If you're in that 3\-5 year range and want a role where you're learning, visible, and actually part of the business rather than just supporting it from the sidelines, this is well worth a conversation. How to Apply To apply for this role, submit your CV via the application form provided. Alternatively, to speak in absolute confidence about this opportunity, please contact Jack Tyrrell via j.tyrrell@mcsgroup.jobs Disclaimer If you have a disability which means you require assistance at any stage of the recruitment process, please contact us directly to discuss. We are committed to providing equality of opportunity to all. Please note, we are receiving an exceptionally high number of applications at present and will be unable to shortlist candidates who are not meeting the specific requirements for this role. Due to the high volume of applications, we may be unable to provide individual feedback. We thank you in advance for your understanding.

title: ITSM Process Owner - Incident & Problem Management
description: Because we strive to put people first. Culture, our way. **Technology at Primark** What’s a career at Primark all about? The positive impact you’re making, the experiences you’re having and the people you’re with. You’re our inspiration. Embrace what sets you apart, own your career and develop in ways you never expected. Leave your mark. And do it your way. Our technology team is actively shaping the next wave of advancements. Engaged with innovative initiatives, your expertise will propel our business into the future. Collaborating with a creative team of tech enthusiasts, you’ll contribute your unique skills to fuel our technological advancements. **What You’ll Do as** **ITSM Process Owner \- Incident \& Problem Management** In your role, you’ll collaborate with different people across a range of skillsets. Here’s a flavour of your day\-to\-day: * Own the end\-to\-end governance, consistency, and continual improvement of one or more ITIL process domains across Primark’s technology estate, ensuring alignment with business needs, ITSM strategy, and compliance across internal teams and third‑party partners, reporting to the Service Assurance Lead. * Act as the accountable owner for assigned ITIL processes (e.g., Incident, Problem, Change Management), defining, documenting, and maintaining policies, standards, workflows, and procedures while ensuring consistent adoption, governance, and compliance against agreed KPIs and SLAs. * Serve as the subject matter expert and escalation point for process\-related issues, supporting integration with ITSM tools such as ServiceNow, identifying optimisation and automation opportunities, and collaborating with Service Desk, Service Owners, Transition Managers, Product Teams, and Engineering to ensure operational supportability. * Define, maintain, and analyse process metrics and KPIs, lead performance and compliance reviews, and deliver regular dashboards and reports to the Service Assurance Lead and stakeholders, highlighting trends, risks, insights, and improvement opportunities. * Identify, prioritise, and implement Continual Service Improvement initiatives to improve maturity, efficiency, and service quality; facilitate improvement workshops and reviews; track progress and outcomes, ensuring alignment with broader ITSM and organisational objectives. * Engage and collaborate with internal service teams, product and engineering functions, and third‑party providers to drive process adherence and shared accountability, promote awareness of process value and behaviours, and support training and change communication initiatives. **What You’ll Get** People are at the heart of what we do here, so it’s essential we provide you with the right environment to perform at your very best. Let’s talk lifestyle: Healthcare, pension, and potential bonus. 27 days of leave, plus bank holidays and if you want, you can buy 5 more. Because Primark is all about tailoring to you, we offer Tax Saver Tickets, fitness centre, and a subsidised cafeteria. **What You’ll Bring** Here at Primark, we want everyone to feel valued – so please bring your authentic self to work, of course with some other key experience and abilities for this role in particular: * 4\+ years of experience in IT Service Management with proven responsibility for managing or owning ITIL processes, supported by a strong understanding of ITIL v4 (or v3\) in enterprise and multi‑supplier environments. * Demonstrated experience implementing, governing, and improving ITSM processes across internal teams and external providers, including managed service providers and SIAM or multi‑vendor operating models. * Proficiency in ITSM platforms such as ServiceNow and associated reporting tools, with the ability to monitor performance, compliance, and improvement opportunities. * Strong analytical and problem‑solving capability, using data‑driven insights to support decision‑making, process optimisation, and continual improvement initiatives. * Excellent communication, collaboration, and stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to influence process adoption, compliance, and behavioural change across diverse teams. * Desirable experience including ITIL v4 Managing Professional or Intermediate certification, retail or distributed business environments, leading CSI or process maturity assessments, and familiarity with Agile or DevOps ways of working and their integration with ITSM processes. Does this sound like you? Great, because we can’t wait to see what you’ll bring. You’ll be supported within a team of equally capable people, celebrating who you are and aiding you reach your potential. At Primark, we’re excited about our future \- and we’re excited to develop yours. **About Primark** At Primark, people matter. They’re the beating heart of our business and the reason we’ve grown from our first store in Dublin in 1969 to a £9bn\+ turnover business and over 80,000 colleagues and over 440 stores in 17 countries today. Our values run through everything we do. In essence, we're Caring and always strive to put people first. We're also Dynamic, bravely pushing the boundaries to stay ahead. And finally, we succeed Together. If you need any reasonable adjustments or have an accessibility request, during your recruitment journey, such as extended time or breaks between online assessments, a sign language interpreter, mobility access, or assistive technology please contact your talent acquisition specialist. All offers of employment are subject to background checks, including right to work, reference education and for some roles criminal, and financial checks. If you have any concerns, please reach out to our talent acquisition team to discuss. **REQ ID:** JR\-8231

title: Independent Consultant in M&A
description: You already have the network. We have the opportunities to match it with. Hetmos Group is a boutique organization active across three premium verticals: M\&A for small and lower mid\-cap companies, strategic and off\-market real estate, and wealth structuring for executives, entrepreneurs, and investors. We are building a community of Independent Mandate Partners to represent our activities within their own ecosystem. *What this partnership looks lik* e You introduce us to high\-value contacts: CEOs, shareholders, investors, HNWIs, strategic partners. You identify opportunities in M\&A, real estate, or wealth. You initiate the relationship. We handle qualification, structuring, negotiation, closing. You are paid a significant commission at closing. No execution on your side. No operational load. Just intelligent, targeted introductions. *The right profile* • Experienced : consultants, bankers, brokers, advisors, lawyers, accountants, family office professionals, senior executives with an active network of decision\-makers • Junior : welcome if introduced, with strong relational skills and real access to qualified people M\&A, real estate, or wealth expertise is not required. We train. *What we offer* • 100% success\-based compensation, uncapped commissions • Independent contractor status, complete freedom • Ability to operate across one, two, or all three verticals • Experienced in\-house execution team • Premium positioning that reflects well on your introductions *Long\-term upside* Top performers access larger mandates, can structure a territory, and may be invited to equity participation. I *nterested?* If your network deserves more than coffees and small talk, talk to us.

title: Industrial Cleaner
description: Job Introduction **Industrial Cleaner** **Location – St James Gate, Dublin 8** **Salary – €15\.80 per hour** **Hours – 40 hours per week** **Shifts – 5 out of 7 days** As a **Industiral** **Cleaner** at Sodexo in **St James Gate**, you are a vital part of a team that helps create a clean, welcoming, and safe environment. Your work contributes to the smooth daily operations of our client’s site, ensuring high standards of hygiene and cleanliness are maintained. If you’re looking for a hands\-on role where you can make a real difference every day, this could be the job for you. **What you’ll do:** * Maintain cleanliness across various areas, including offices, restrooms, and communal spaces * Ensure high standards of hygiene are consistently met * Replenish supplies such as soap, hand towels, and toilet paper * Follow health and safety procedures, including COSHH regulations * Report any maintenance or cleaning issues promptly **What you bring:** * A can\-do attitude and pride in delivering high\-quality cleaning * Ability to work both independently and as part of a team * Good time management and reliability * Flexibility to work shifts across Monday to Sunday * No prior qualifications required – just a strong work ethic and attention to detail **What We Offer** Working with our company is more than a job; it’s a chance to be part of something greater. You’ll belong in a company and team that values you for who you are; you’ll act with purpose and have an impact through your everyday actions; and you’ll be able to thrive in your own way. In addition, we offer: * Sodexo benefits * Flexible and dynamic work environment * Access to ongoing training and development programs * Countless opportunities to grow within the company * Full training supplied **Ready to be part of something greater? Apply today!** **About Sodexo** At Sodexo, our purpose is to create a better every day for everyone to build a better life for all. We operate in 55 countries, serving over 100 million consumers each day through our unique combination of On\-Site Food and FM Services, Benefits \& Rewards Services and Personal \& Home Services. We are committed to being an inclusive employer. We are a forces\-friendly employer. We welcome and encourage applications from people with a diverse variety of experiences, backgrounds, and identities. We encourage our employees to get involved with our Employee Networks such as Pride, Sodexo Parents \& Carers, Sodexo Disability, Ability network, So Together, Generations and Origins. We’re a Disability Confident Leader employer. We’re committed to changing attitudes towards disability and making sure disabled people have the chance to fulfil their aspirations.

title: Installation Engineer
description: **Who We Are** Applied Materials is a global leader in materials engineering solutions used to produce virtually every new chip and advanced display in the world. We design, build and service cutting\-edge equipment that helps our customers manufacture display and semiconductor chips – the brains of devices we use every day. As the foundation of the global electronics industry, Applied enables the exciting technologies that literally connect our world – like AI and IoT. If you want to push the boundaries of materials science and engineering to create next generation technology, join us to deliver material innovation that changes the world. **What We Offer** Location: Dublin,IRL You’ll benefit from a supportive work culture that encourages you to learn, develop, and grow your career as you take on challenges and drive innovative solutions for our customers. We empower our team to push the boundaries of what is possible—while learning every day in a supportive leading global company. Visit our Careers website to learn more. At Applied Materials, we care about the health and wellbeing of our employees. We’re committed to providing programs and support that encourage personal and professional growth and care for you at work, at home, or wherever you may go. Learn more about our benefits. As a Field Service Engineer \[Customer Engineer] at Applied Materials, you serve as the direct liaison with customers, collaborating closely to install, maintain, and upgrade equipment. You'll use digital analytics for troubleshooting and apply basic diagnostic techniques to assess and address technical issues. Your responsibilities include performing preventative and corrective maintenance on various systems such as electrical, vacuum, mechanical, plasma, hydraulic, and gas systems. Additionally, you'll coordinate and communicate directly with customers to ensure smooth operations and exceptional service. In order to support our long\-term development, we are looking for talents to join our teams in Europe (Dresden, Germany / Dublin, Ireland / Grenoble, France). Within our Installation department, we are looking for our **Installation Engineer to support European regions** . The iTeam (Installation team) stands for the Installation of semiconductors production equipment across the European Region (mainly in Germany, ireland, France and Italy). We roll in, assemble, connect, and test the new systems until we hand over to our service team and our customers for the manufacturing of wafers to kick off. Being part of the Install Task Force is more than a job, it is a way of life. As a team **we travel approx. 75 % of our working time** , and also get opportunities to support installations worldwide. This position demands technical expertise, customer focus, team involvement and flexibility to travel. **Full training on our equipment will be provided through mentoring.** **Key Responsibilities** * Perform independently installation tasks (mechanical assembly, electrical connections, robot calibrations), from the integration to the inspection of the tools including troubleshooting in case of issues. * Follow all checklists and procedures and ensure correct procedures and schematics are available prior to performing work. * Test equipment with assistance from senior engineers, existing documentation and diagnostic techniques. * Respond to factory issues by communicating with other engineers and participating in escalation conference calls. * Participate in continuous improvement projects to reduce installation cycle time and costs. * Provide accurate reporting of status, hours worked and non\-conformances. * Adhere to the security policy and quality standards of Applied Materials and its customers. **Skills and Experience required:** * Bachelor or Master degree with technical background (mechanical assembly, equipment installation, maintenance, mechatronics, electronics, electrical, mechanical, or similar...) * Position open to junior profiles. * Troubleshooting skills to solve mechanical and electrical issues during system installation. * Ability to communicate daily pass\-downs and equipment status reports. * Project management skills to control installation project within budget, timeline and installation quality requirements. * Ability to work autonomously. * Good English communication skills both written and verbal. * Good Knowledge of the MS Office (Excel, Powerpoint) * The position requires extensive travelling throughout Europe (75% of the time, depending on overall activity requirements) **Physical Requirements:** This position often requires extensive walking, standing for extended periods of time, working in tight spaces, bending, squatting, twisting, kneeling, reaching, climbing stairs and ladders, working at height, lifting up to 35 lbs., the ability to distinguish between colors and working with electricity, the ability to work in an environment with noise that may be difficult for some individuals with sensitivity to noise, and the use of hand and power tools. Additionally, because this position generally involves working in a clean room, it requires the use of appropriate Personal Protective Equipment such as coveralls, hoods, booties, safety glasses, gloves, respirators, chemical aprons, and face shields. **Additional Information** **Time Type:** Full time **Employee Type:** Assignee / Regular **Travel:** Yes, 75% of the Time **Relocation Eligible:** No Applied Materials is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, national origin, citizenship, ancestry, religion, creed, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, disability, veteran or military status, or any other basis prohibited by law.

We design, build and service cutting\-edge equipment that helps our customers manufacture display and semiconductor chips – the brains of devices we use every day. title: Integration Engineer
description: At U.S. Bank, we’re on a journey to do our best. Helping the customers and businesses we serve to make better and smarter financial decisions, enabling the communities we support to grow and succeed in the right ways, all more confidently and more often—that’s what we call the courage to thrive. We believe it takes all of us to bring our shared ambition to life, and each person is unique in their potential. A career with U.S. Bank gives you a wide, ever\-growing range of opportunities to discover what makes you thrive. Try new things, learn new skills and discover what you excel at—all from Day One. As a wholly owned subsidiary of U.S. Bank, Elavon is committed to building the platforms and ecosystems that help over 1\.5 million customers around the world to achieve their financial goals—no matter what they need. From transaction processing to customer service, to driving innovation and launching new products, we’re building a range of tailored payment solutions powered by the latest technology. As part of our team, you can explore what motivates and energizes your career goals: partnering with our customers, our communities, and each other. We actively uphold transparent and fair hiring practices that support individual opportunity, inclusive culture, and career mobility across all levels of our organisation. We offer meaningful opportunities for growth, a culture of inclusion, and a strong commitment to transparency and integrity in everything we do. **Job Description** **Integration Engineer** We are seeking an experienced API Engineer to design, build, secure, and operate enterprise\-grade APIs that integrate cloud platforms with on‑premise banking application suites. The role focuses on developing reliable and scalable integration services, enabling secure data exchange across channels and systems (core banking, payments, fraud, risk, CRM, digital platforms), while meeting stringent regulatory, availability, and security requirements. You will work closely with solution architects, platform engineers, security teams, and business stakeholders to deliver API products aligned to bank integration patterns, including synchronous REST services and event\-driven integrations. **Direct line reporting:** * Head of Architecture and Core Banking – Europe. * Accountabilities and Responsibilities **API Design \& Delivery** * Design and implement RESTful APIs and integration services connecting cloud services to on‑prem banking systems. * Create reusable API patterns for common banking capabilities (customer, accounts, payments, transactions, entitlements). * Define and maintain API contracts (OpenAPI/Swagger), versioning strategies, and backward compatibility. * Deliver APIs that meet performance, latency, availability, and resiliency expectations (e.g., timeouts, retries, circuit breakers). **Security, Compliance \& Controls** * Implement strong API security controls: OAuth2/OIDC, mTLS, JWT validation, request signing, API gateway policies, and rate limiting. * Apply secure coding practices and align solutions with bank security standards, audit requirements, and regulatory expectations (e.g., least privilege, encryption in transit/at rest). * Collaborate with security to support threat modeling, pen test remediation, and secure configuration management. **Cloud–On‑Prem Connectivity \& Integration** * Build and operate integration flows using enterprise tooling (examples: API gateway, service mesh, ESB/iPaaS, message brokers). * Implement hybrid connectivity patterns such as VPN/ExpressRoute/Direct Connect equivalents, private endpoints, network segmentation, and secure routing. * Integrate with on‑prem technologies (e.g., MQ, legacy services, SOAP where required, databases, batch systems) and modern cloud services. **Observability \& Operations** * Implement observability: structured logging, distributed tracing, metrics, dashboards, and alerting. * Support production deployments and incident triage; conduct root cause analysis and introduce reliability improvements. * Automate CI/CD pipelines and Infrastructure/Configuration as Code where applicable. **Engineering Excellence** * Write clean, testable code (unit/integration/contract tests). * Participate in code reviews, technical design reviews, and architecture governance. * Document API products and integration runbooks for support and operational readiness. * Experience and Key Competencies: **Knowledge \& Experience:** * 5\+ years (adjust as needed) developing APIs/integration services in regulated enterprise environments; banking or financial services experience strongly preferred. * BA/MBA in IT or relevant experience * Proven experience building hybrid integrations: cloud to on‑prem application connectivity and secure data exchange. * Strong hands\-on experience with API design, implementation, and lifecycle management. **Technical Skills** * Strong proficiency in one or more of: Java (Spring Boot), .NET, Node.js, or Python for API development. * Strong experience with API gateways (e.g., Apigee, Kong, Azure API Management, AWS API Gateway, Mulesoft, etc.). * Experience with messaging/eventing platforms: Kafka, MQ, RabbitMQ, Azure Service Bus, etc. * Experience with containerization and orchestration: Docker, Kubernetes/OpenShift. * Strong understanding of integration patterns: sync/async, pub/sub, idempotency, retries, backpressure, saga/compensation, and data consistency. * Strong understanding of data formats (JSON, XML, Avro) and schema evolution. **Security \& Quality** * Practical experience implementing OAuth2/OIDC, mTLS, certificate management, secrets management (e.g., Vault, Key Vault, Secrets Manager). * Experience with SDLC controls: SAST/DAST, dependency scanning, secure CI pipelines. * Test automation: unit tests, integration tests, consumer\-driven contract testing (e.g., Pact). **Personal Attributes** * Highly organised, proactive, and detail‑oriented, with the ability to operate at pace. * Comfortable operating in ambiguity and adapting to changing priorities. * Resilient, calm under pressure, and confident acting as an extension of the CIO. * Trusted partner with a collaborative, inclusive leadership style. * Banking domain knowledge: payments (SEPA/SWIFT), card processing, transaction monitoring, AML/KYC, customer onboarding. * Exposure to 4th generation banking platforms such as (Thought Machine or 10X) * Experience with service mesh (Istio/Linkerd) and zero trust patterns. * Familiarity with TOGAF, enterprise integration architecture, or API product management. * Experience with data privacy and retention requirements (GDPR), and operational controls. **Key Competencies:** **Communications \& Relationships:** * Excellent communication skills required to manage third party relationships. Ability to use insights and relationships to influence and deliver. The ability to build connections and partnerships within own business area and beyond, through formal and informal channels, utilising considerable tact, diplomacy and people skills. **Agility and innovation:** * Adapt quickly to changing priorities; create new and better ways for the organization to be successful. **Collaboration:** * Build partnerships and promote a culture of teamwork to drive on U.S. Bank. **Drive for results:** * Hold self and others accountable to consistently achieve meaningful results. **Ethics and trust:** * Maintain high ethical standards; gain the confidence and trust of others through honesty, integrity and authenticity. **Inclusion:** * Build a culture of equity and inclusion in which diverse ideas, talent and perspectives are welcomed and encouraged. **Customer centricity:** * Build strong customer relationships and deliver customer\-centric solutions. **Commercial Judgement:** * Strong knowledge of business unit with the ability to understand USBE’s business and deliver the business performance needed to achieve USBE’s purpose and goals. **Managing Programs:** * Proven analytical, decision\-making and problem\-solving skills, with the ability to plan and implement appropriate measures to mitigate the impact of an event affecting USBE’s ability to achieve its objectives. To complete day\-to\-day activities in a manner which is consistent with legal, regulatory and stakeholder expectations. **3 Conduct Standards \& Rules** * You must act with professionalism and integrity * You must act with due skill and diligence * You must be open and cooperative with the Central Bank of Ireland and other regulators * You must disclose appropriately any information of which the Central Bank of Ireland would reasonably expect notice **Location expectations** This role requires working from a U.S. Bank location three (3\) or more days per week. **Accessibility** We are committed to providing an inclusive and accessible recruitment experience. If you need adjustments at any stage of the application or hiring process, please contact your recruiter for guidance and support. **Total Rewards** U.S. Bank is committed to fair, equitable, and transparent compensation practices in line with local regulatory and legal requirements. Our total rewards approach is designed to attract, retain, and support top talent while ensuring equal pay for work of equal value. We offer a market\-competitive compensation package that includes: * Clearly defined salary ranges aligned with industry benchmarks and internal equity standards. * Performance\-based incentives for eligible employees (as defined by relevant plan rules), awarded through transparent, objective criteria that recognize both individual and company performance. * Inclusive equitable benefits that are accessible to all employees and focused around our 3 main pillars of financial wellbeing, health \& wellness). * Continuous development opportunities including training, education support, and career progression pathways based on inclusive and transparent criteria. * Employee recognition programs that celebrate achievements and milestones for all. We regularly review our compensation and benefits to ensure they remain competitive, inclusive, and responsive to employee needs and market trends. Further details of the compensation package will be provided upon application. We encourage candidates to explore the full value of our offer, including monetary and non\-monetary benefits, at Employee benefits and development \| U.S. Bank \| Elavon. **Closing Date** Posting may be closed earlier due to high volume of applicants. We aim to provide timely updates throughout the process and encourage early applications to ensure consideration.

title: Internship
description: **Descrição da Empresa** Sustentabilidade Agora é uma empresa inovadora criada com a missão de simplificar a sustentabilidade para indivíduos e organizações. Utilizamos soluções tecnológicas e informações de alta qualidade para apoiar empresas a alcançar metas relacionadas a ESG e promover estilos de vida mais sustentáveis. Nosso trabalho está alinhado com a visão de criar um impacto positivo tanto para os negócios quanto para o meio ambiente. Se você deseja contribuir para um mundo mais sustentável, nós somos o lugar certo para você. **Descrição do Cargo** Procuramos uma pessoa estagiária para integrar nossa equipe. As principais responsabilidades incluem apoiar na implementação de inteligência artificial, contribuir com soluções digitais e inovação e aprender na prática em um ambiente dinâmico. Este cargo é uma posição de estágio, ***em modelo remoto*** , ideal para quem deseja iniciar uma carreira na área de sustentabilidade corporativa. **Qualificações** * Estudantes de Sistemas de Informação ou áreas afins * Perfil proativo e com vontade de aprender * Interesse por tecnologia aplicada a impacto * Conhecimento em Sustentabilidade é um grande diferencial! * Capacidade de comunicar\-se de forma clara e eficaz, além de habilidade para colaborar com equipes e parceiros externos. * Competência para elaborar relatórios e realizar análises relacionadas a sustentabilidade e inteligência artificial. * Desejável possuir um bom nível de inglês/espanhol.

title: Internship Officer (Studio+) – Creative Futures Academy / NCAD (0.5 FTE)
description: **Post Title \& Subject Area** **Internship Officer (Studio\+) Creative Futures Academy / NCAD (0\.5 FTE)** **Grade:** Grade V **Post Duration:** 15\-month specified purpose funded contract **Reporting Relationship:** Reports to: Head of Department \- Innovation \& Engagement **Salary:** Grade V salary scale 52,240 62,482 per annum pro\-rata (26,120 31,241\) (Standard public sector terms and entry at minimum point apply) **Location:** NCAD, 100 Thomas Street, Dublin 8 **Hours of Work:** 17\.5 hours per week (net of breaks) **Annual Leave:** 25 days pro\-rata (12\.5 days), exclusive of public holidays **Role Overview:** NCAD seeks to appoint an Internship Officer to support the delivery, coordination, and continuous enhancement of the Studio\+ internship and real\-world learning model within the Creative Futures Academy. This role is central to enabling high\-quality, practice\-based learning experiences that connect students with the creative industries. It combines industry engagement, academic coordination, student support, and operational delivery. The postholder will be responsible for developing and sustaining a pipeline of relevant internship opportunities; ensuring alignment between placements and academic learning outcomes; coordinating assessment processes; and capturing the impact of internships through structured case studies and reporting. The role requires a candidate who can operate effectively across academic and professional contexts, with strong organisational capability, stakeholder management skills, and an understanding of creative sector practices. **Key Responsibilities:** **1\. Internship Development and Industry Engagement** * Source, develop, and maintain a diverse pipeline of high\-quality internship opportunities across the creative and cultural sectors. \[DMcG1] * Build and sustain relationships with industry partners, public bodies, and cultural organisations. * Act as a key liaison between NCAD and external stakeholders, ensuring clear communication of expectations, processes, and mutual value. **2\. Academic Alignment and Quality Assurance** * Work closely with academic staff to ensure internships align with programme learning outcomes and assessment frameworks. * Support the development and implementation of learning agreements and assessment criteria. * Monitor the quality of internship experiences and address issues in a timely and structured manner. **3\. Student Engagement and Support** * Guide students in sourcing, selecting, and preparing for internships appropriate to their discipline and career ambitions. * Deliver briefings, workshops, and induction sessions on internship processes, expectations, and professional practice. * Support students in setting learning objectives and engaging in reflective practice throughout their placement. * Provide ongoing support during internships, including managing challenges and referring students to appropriate supports where necessary. **4\. Assessment and Module Coordination** * Coordinate the delivery of internship\-related assessment processes in collaboration with academic staff. * Support the preparation and dissemination of assignment briefs aligned with module learning outcomes. * Manage and maintain internship modules on Canvas (or equivalent LMS), including submission points, communications, and tracking of student engagement. * Oversee the collection, organisation, and collation of student submissions for academic review and assessment. **5\. Operational Delivery and Administration** * Manage end\-to\-end internship processes, including opportunity promotion, approvals, documentation, and reporting. * Ensure all placements are supported by appropriate documentation (e.g. learning agreements, memoranda of understanding where required). * Maintain accurate and auditable records of internships, student participation, and partner engagement. * Ensure compliance with institutional and regulatory requirements, including health and safety, insurance, and data protection. **6\. Systems Development and Continuous Improvement** * Develop and maintain clear, scalable workflows, guidance materials, and documentation for students, staff, and partners. * Contribute to the standardisation and continuous improvement of internship processes across Studio\+ programmes. * Gather and analyse feedback from students, staff, and partners to enhance the effectiveness and efficiency of delivery. * Support reporting requirements related to programme evaluation, accreditation, and strategic planning. **7\. Case Study Development and Impact Reporting** * Develop structured internship case studies that capture the full lifecycle and impact of internship experiences. * Synthesise information from internship job descriptions, student outputs (including learning logs and reflective journals) (anonymised), and employer feedback. * Produce clear, high\-quality summaries that articulate: * + the nature of the placement and student contribution + the skills and competencies developed + the value of the experience to both student and host organisation * Contribute to the use of these case studies as strategic assets for: * + student recruitment and programme promotion + industry engagement and partnership development + evidencing the impact of Studio\+ within the creative sector **Core Competencies** The successful candidate will demonstrate: * Strong understanding of the creative industries and professional practice contexts * Proven ability to build and manage external partnerships * Experience coordinating complex, multi\-stakeholder programmes or processes * Ability to ensure quality and consistency in work\-based learning environments * Strong organisational and administrative capability, with attention to detail * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills * A student\-centred approach with a focus on employability and professional development * Capacity to develop structured processes and improve systems over time * Proficiency in digital platforms, particularly learning management systems (e.g. Canvas) **Selection Criteria** **Essential** * A minimum of: * + (a) 3 years relevant experience in a similar role **and** a relevant qualification (NFQ Level 6 or higher), **or** + (b) 5 years relevant professional experience * Demonstrable experience working with external stakeholders or partners * Strong organisational, coordination, and administrative skills * Experience supporting programme delivery, training, or work\-based learning initiatives * Excellent written and verbal communication skills * Ability to work independently and collaboratively across teams **Desirable** * Experience in a higher education environment, particularly in art, design, or creative disciplines * Experience coordinating internships, placements, or experiential learning modules * Experience developing industry\-academic partnerships or live project briefs * Experience producing case studies, reports, or impact\-focused documentation * Familiarity with Canvas or similar LMS platforms * Understanding of graduate employability and practice\-based education models **Summary** This role is critical to the successful delivery and ongoing development of Studio\+ at NCAD. It requires a balance of operational efficiency, academic coordination, industry engagement, and strategic insight. The Internship Officer will play a key role in ensuring that internship experiences are high\-quality, well\-structured, and impactful, while also capturing and communicating their value to support the continued growth and reputation of NCADs practice\-based learning model. **Closing Date: 20th of May 2026**

title: Investment Analyst- 18 Month FTC
description: **About Us** At Davy, it's the unique talents of all our people that have been the foundations of our success for 100 years. As we continue to grow, so do you. Because you are not just part of our team \- you are a key player in shaping our future. At Davy, you are the difference. Established in 1926, the Davy Group sits at the heart of wealth and capital in Ireland, providing a broad range of services to high\-net\-worth individuals, self\-directed investors, small\-to\-medium enterprises, credit unions, corporations, and domestic \& international institutional investors. Our culture is shaped around three values, which are at the heart of everything we do: client success, one Davy, and building a proud legacy. Sharing the same goals, we work together to deliver exceptional outcomes for our clients. As part of a team of over 900, you will be encouraged to learn and to grow, both professionally and personally. **Requirements** **About the role** An exciting opportunity has opened up in our Investment Team. In the role, the successful candidate will: * Work with the senior members on monitoring and reporting of performance and risk of funds and portfolios managed by Davy to internal and external benchmarks; * Analyzing and effectively communicating the drivers of this performance to the different stakeholders including client advisors ; * Monitor risk exposures across portfolios, including market, credit, and liquidity risks. * Ensuring our processes are robust by reviewing and updating the procedures, ensuring appropriate controls are used, developing relevant metrics and cross\-training with other analysts where needed; * Collating documentation in relation to portfolio and funds for various investment committees; * Supporting the Investment Committee's evaluations and decisions with ad hoc investment analysis related to the current or prospective products; * Manage relationships with external stakeholders including the boards of funds managed by Davy; * Working with the broader investment team to improve existing and creation of new fund management and operational processes; * Monitoring of funds allocations and trades in line with investment policies; * Assisting asset allocation analytics to ensure suitability, cost minimization \& implementation; * Working with our colleagues in IT to drive efficiencies in processes and production. * Participate in change forums and project meetings, providing input, sharing feedback and supporting testing activities to ensure successful implementation of change initiatives. F\&P The position set out above is defined in the Fitness \& Probity Standards issued by the Central Bank of Ireland as a "Controlled Function (CF)". In compliance with the Fitness and Probity Standards applicable to your position, any offer of employment is conditional upon you demonstrating that you meet the Fitness \& Probity Standards. **About You** We are looking for somebody who has the following background and skills: * 5\+ years of relevant experience * Bachelor's/Master's degree in Economics/Finance/Mathematics etc * Familiar with multi\-asset portfolios and institutional investment strategies. * Experience of quantitative research, performance measurement, performance attribution and ex\-ante/ex\-post risk * Strong interest in financial modelling , portfolio analytics, statistical analysis and data modeling; * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills, written and verbal communication skills. * Working knowledge of scripted/programming languages (eg, Python and SQL) ; * Strong understanding of financial markets and the main asset classes; * Excellent information technology capability, including a high level of competence in Microsoft Office products (particularly Excel, Outlook, Word, PowerPoint); * Knowledge of UCITS, MiFID II or SFDR regulatory requirements would be beneficial. * CFA qualification, or working towards, would be beneficial; * Excellent planning and organizational capabilities **Benefits** **What we offer** At Davy, our people are our biggest investment. In today's fast\-moving world, we know it's important to feel secure and empowered. That's why Davy benefits support you through all of life's stages, offering health and wellness, flexible working options, flexible benefits allowance, learning and development opportunities, through to assisted retirement planning. **Health and Wellbeing** Psychological safety, emotional wellbeing and support are a priority at Davy. We offer a comprehensive Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) and mental health first aid. Our Sports \& Social teams promote physical wellbeing and social connection. **Reward and Recognition** We offer a competitive remuneration package to include salary, bonus, pension and a host of additional benefits to protect you and your family when it matters most. **Growth and Development** Our award\-winning L\&D function supports you and your growth at Davy. We offer a comprehensive range of professional and personal development opportunities. Our culture supports learning, and we encourage you to explore more at Davy. **Positive Workplace** Our workplace is inclusive, diverse, and offers everyone a sense of belonging. We are flexible and continue to support a hybrid working model. You will get to be your best at Davy. **Values and Culture** Exceptional client outcomes delivered by a world class team, underpinned by our core Davy values \- One Team, Client Success, and Proud Legacy. **Important Information** Davy is an equal opportunities employer, committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse workplace. We value diversity in both background and experience, and even if you don't meet all of the requirements outlined in the job description, we still encourage you to apply. We review all applications received. As part of our commitment to creating an accessible environment, we want to ensure that everyone has an equal opportunity to participate in the interview process. If you require any reasonable accommodations, please let us know, and we will do our best to make the necessary arrangements. Appointment to this role is subject to the candidate's eligibility to work in Ireland. Where agency assistance is required, our Recruitment Team will engage directly with suppliers. Unsolicited CVs / profiles supplied to us by recruitment agencies will not be accepted for this role. Davy Group is a member of the Bank of Ireland Group.

title: Investment Associate
description: We are currently working with a leading Wealth Management firm who are looking to hire an **Investment Associate** to join their team in Dublin. The ideal candidate will have an interest and understanding of investments and a strong client focus. Responsibilities of this **Investment Associate** Job: * Assist in the creation and execution of relationship plans for each client relating to the implementation of their financial plan, adherence with service standards which lead to the development of long\-term client relationships * Identifying revenue potential and opportunities to cross\-sell the firm's services * Execute an engagement plan for each advisory relationship to ensure they receive proactive, relevant and appropriate contact, investment ideas and recommendations and structured portfolio reviews * Deal promptly and efficiently with client queries * Review existing client relationships, and in line with Investment Strategy, analyse whether the relationships are categorised correctly and therefore managed appropriately Requirements of this **Investment Associate** Job: * Degree or Masters in Business related area * 2\-3 years industry experience * QFA qualification * Demonstrable interest and understanding of investments * Strong communication and presentation skills This is an excellent opportunity for someone looking to continue to develop their career in Wealth Management. For more information on this **Investment Associate** job or to discuss in more detail, contact Aoife Stokes at astokes@elevatepartners.ie or apply directly below.

title: Investment Operations Specialist - 12 Month Contract
description: **Company:** ------------ Mercer **Description:** ---------------- Mercer’s Investment Management business, Investment Solutions International (IS), is looking for a motivated and detail\-oriented investment operations specialist to join our Operations team. **This is a 12 month fixed term contract role with potential for permanency subject to performance and business needs** . While prior experience in regulatory reporting or fund expense oversight is a plus, it is not required. We are looking for someone with a basic understanding of fund accounting and a keen interest in learning about European investment products and operational processes. **What can you expect:** Mercer is a fast\-growing investment solutions provider with a broad and expanding client base. Our fund range includes approximately 140 Irish domiciled funds across multiple legal structures, supporting Mercer’s investment and risk management solutions for institutional clients. The Operations team supports a variety of important activities including onboarding new clients, launching funds, managing fund rebalancing, overseeing NAV and valuations, and supporting regulatory and tax compliance. You will work closely with experienced colleagues and third\-party service providers to help ensure smooth and accurate operations. The Regulatory Reporting team focuses on ensuring Mercer meets its regulatory obligations, including reporting under MiFID II, AIFMD, MMFR, EMIR, and PRIIPs KIDs. As an Associate, you will become responsible for reporting processes and gain exposure to regulatory changes and process improvement. In addition, you will support Fund Expenses Oversight liaising closely with our Dublin lead and Mumbai team to ensure all fund expense related tasks are completed accurately and on time. This role offers a great learning environment where you will support \& contribute to key operational and regulatory tasks, develop your knowledge of investment operations, and actively work on team projects. **We will rely on you to:** * Learn and develop a solid understanding of Mercer’s fund structures and investment solutions * Support the team in delivering accurate and timely regulatory reporting and fund expense oversight * Act as primary subject matter expert and support for offshore colleagues. * Assist with data validation, monitoring, and basic analysis to ensure data quality * Help research and summarize regulatory requirements and fund operations under guidance * Support process improvements and automation initiatives to enhance operational efficiency, driving forward solutions and taking ownership of outcomes. * Collaborate with internal teams and external service providers to support daily operations * Contribute to team projects and support the implementation of new regulatory requirements * Maintain a strong focus on risk management and compliance within your work **What you will need to have:** * 2\-5 years of experience in asset management, fund administration, or investment operations, with relevant academic background (Business, Accounting, Finance, Economics). * Good understanding of fund accounting principles and investment products. * Strong attention to detail and willingness to learn. * Excellent analytical skills and comfort working with data (Excel proficiency required; SQL or other data tools a plus). * Interest in regulatory frameworks and compliance processes (prior experience is a bonus but not essential). * Effective communication skills and ability to work collaboratively in a team environment. * Self\-motivated, self\-starter with a positive attitude and eagerness to develop professionally. * Ability to manage multiple tasks and work in a fast\-paced environment. * High performance outlook comfortable taking ownership of work and driving solutions. * Good technology skills including Microsoft Office (Excel, PowerPoint, Word) and openness to learning new tools. **What will make you stand out:** * Enthusiasm for building a career in investment operations and regulatory reporting * Proactive approach to learning and problem\-solving * Strong organizational skills and ability to deliver work accurately and on time * Excellent verbal and written communication skills * Ability to quickly absorb new information and adapt to change * Confident and comfortable in making decisions. **Why join our team:** * To be part of a company with a strong brand, committed to making a difference in people's lives. * We help you be your best through professional development opportunities, interesting work and supportive leaders. * We foster a vibrant and inclusive culture where you can work with talented colleagues to create new solutions and have impact for colleagues, clients and communities. * Our scale enables us to provide a range of career opportunities, as well as benefits and rewards to enhance your well\-being. \#MercerIS Mercer is a business of Marsh (NYSE: MRSH), a global leader in risk, reinsurance and capital, people and investments, and management consulting, advising clients in 130 countries. With annual revenue of over $27 billion and more than 95,000 colleagues, Marsh helps build the confidence to thrive through the power of perspective. For more information about Mercer, visit mercer.com, or follow us on LinkedIn and X. ### **Marsh is committed to embracing a diverse, inclusive and flexible work environment. We aim to attract and retain the best people and embrace diversity of age, background, disability, ethnic origin, family duties, gender orientation or expression, marital status, nationality, parental status, personal or social status, political affiliation, race, religion and beliefs, sex/gender, sexual orientation or expression, skin color, Traveller community, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law. We are an equal opportunities employer. We are committed to providing reasonable support to any candidate with a disability/health condition to allow them to fully participate in the recruitment process. We welcome candidates to contact us at reasonableaccommodations@mmc.com to discuss any specific needs.** ### **Marsh is committed to hybrid work, which includes the flexibility of working remotely and the collaboration, connections and professional development benefits of working together in the office. All Marsh colleagues are expected to be in their local office or working onsite with clients at least three days per week. Office\-based teams will identify at least one “anchor day” per week on which their full team will be together in person.**

Fitness classes, Mindfulness Workshops, Employee Assistance Programme and much more. Generous holiday allowance, enhanced maternity/paternity leave, marriage/civil partnership leave and special leave policies * Educational assistance, incentivised certifications, and accreditations, including AWS, Microsoft, Oracle, and Red Hat * Reward schemes including Version 1’s Annual Excellence Awards \& ‘Call\-Out’ platform. * Environment, Social and Community First initiatives allow you to get involved in local fundraising and development opportunities as part of fostering our diversity, inclusion and belonging schemes. And many more exciting benefits… drop us a note to find out more. Version 1 is an equal opportunities employer. We are committed to building a diverse, inclusive and respectful workplace where everyone feels valued and able to thrive. title: Junior Accounts Payable Administrator
description: **Are you looking to become part of a collaborative team whilst maximising your potential to grow and develop within your career? Apply today.** **The Role.** GMC Utilities Group Ltd are seeking an experienced and detail\-oriented **Junior** **Accounts Payable Administrator** to join our growing Finance Team, based in Ballycoolin, Dublin 11\. This is a key role within the organisation, supporting the Finance Department in managing subcontractor accounts payable processes, ensuring accuracy, efficiency, and compliance in all financial transactions. **Key Duties and Responsibilities will include but are not limited to:** **Key Responsibilities:** Upload weekly subcontractor costs into Sage, creating purchase order cost accruals. Process large volumes of subcontractor invoices against accrued costs, deducting any necessary retentions. Maintain accurate subcontractor creditor balances at all times. Ensure all agreed credit terms are correctly recorded and utilised on the accounting system. Complete creditor statement reconciliations on a regular basis. Prepare weekly and monthly payment lists using aged creditor listings and notes on Sage 200 for review and approval. Notify payments through ROS and process any required RCT deductions within Sage. Review and adjust accrued costs to ensure accuracy. Handle supplier and subcontractor queries promptly and professionally. **The Candidate: Required Experience and Competencies:** Proven experience in an Accounts Payable or Finance Administration role. Strong organisational skills and attention to detail. Excellent communication and interpersonal skills with the ability to collaborate across departments. Flexible approach and ability to manage variable workloads. Strong IT skills, particularly Microsoft Excel (advanced level) and Sage 200 experience (desirable). Fluent English, both written and verbal. **Become a part of something bigger.** At the heart of GMC is our people, from engineers through to customer service and we aim to work collaboratively towards our common goals. GMC is an equal opportunity employer, and we aim to recruit and retain like\-minded people from diverse backgrounds with a wide range of expertise. In GMC we foster a work culture that promotes collaboration and learning, building an environment where our people have the capacity to maximise their potential whilst gaining the opportunity to continuously develop. **Our Future.** Since the founding of GMC in 1981 by Gerry McCloskey, GMC has grown to be Ireland's leading Civils and Utilities company and the future for GMC is bright. We have engaged with our staff and other stakeholders to agree and publish our core values. One of our core values is to *"Focus on the Future"* . Our growth to date reflects the confidence that our customers have in our service delivery, in the innovative approach that we bring to how we do our work, and to our culture of safety and quality. We have ambitious plans to develop our footprint in Ireland and overseas but understand that every decision we make contributes to people's quality of life throughout generations, and we don't take that responsibility lightly as we build Ireland's infrastructure of tomorrow, today.
